title: Rail transport operatives
description: Operate underground loading or moving machine to load or move coal, ore, or rock using shuttle or mine car or conveyors. Equipment may include power shovels, hoisting engines equipped with cable-drawn scraper or scoop, or machines equipped with gathering arms and conveyor. Operate or maintain stationary engines, boilers, or other mechanical equipment to provide utilities for buildings or industrial processes. Operate equipment such as steam engines, generators, motors, turbines, and steam boilers. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Respond to emergency conditions or breakdowns, following applicable safety procedures and rules.
Confer with conductors and other workers via radiotelephones or computers to exchange switching information and report delays or rail problems.
Provide assistance in aligning drawbars and coupling or uncoupling cars, using available equipment and hand signals.
Inspect track and locomotives for defects such as broken rails, damaged parts, or safety compliance, and report findings.
Operate equipment such as locomotives and stationary engines to transport freight or provide utilities for buildings and industrial processes.
Monitor gauges and signals to ensure proper operation of trains and machinery, including checking levels of fuel, water, and other supplies.
Drive machines and equipment to load or move materials such as coal, ore, or rock into cars or onto conveyors, following specifications.
Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine operators, ensuring safety and efficiency in operations.
Prepare reports regarding any problems encountered, including accidents, mechanical issues, or service needs, and document operations.
Perform routine maintenance and repairs on equipment and machinery, including cleaning, lubricating, and making necessary adjustments.
Inspect equipment, including air brakes and couplings, to ensure they are securely fastened and functioning properly.
Activate track switches, observe signals from crew members, and communicate information to engineers to ensure safe and efficient operations.
Monitor loading processes and verify accuracy of cargo records, including weights, tonnages, and handling requirements.
Inspect each car periodically during runs, including checking documentation regarding contents and destinations.
Clean hoppers and other equipment, and handle spillage from tracks and conveyors.
Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure safety, compliance, and lack of defects.
Requisition needed personnel, supplies, equipment, parts, or repair services.
Recommend or implement personnel actions, including selection, evaluation, or disciplinary measures.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, or accidents.
Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers and assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Interpret regulations, safety rules, and company policies for workers to ensure compliance.
Review orders, schedules, and notices to determine work sequences and material shipping details.
Resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.
Enforce safety rules and regulations to maintain a safe working environment.
Direct and coordinate activities of workers in material transportation and related services.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
Participate in continuing education to stay aware of industry trends and developments.
